pull.title: guix: CMake-related improvements
pull.body:
This PR introduces the following CMake-related improvements to [`contrib/guix/libexec/build.sh`](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/contrib/guix/libexec/build.sh):

1. Drop redundant CMake `--verbose` options, as verbose output is already controlled by the [`VERBOSE`](https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/envvar/VERBOSE.html) environment variable, which has been exported since  85f4a4b0822e3aa10310c4623eff719f301e9263.

2. Fixes the "Ignoring empty string" CMake warning for non-Linux hosts.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35503. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Stale ACK | [fanquake](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35503#issuecomment-4689501035) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#35499](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35499) (guix: add `package.sh` by fanquake) * [#25573](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/25573) (guix: produce a `-static-pie` bitcoind by fanquake) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

There's still an instance of `--verbose` in build.sh:
https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/d0b8d445fb7a936d52f88c7d526184f97e462fa2/contrib/guix/libexec/build.sh#L293

@fanquake 

Thank you for the review. Your feedback has been addressed.
ACK 6d963c83bb11168212b4878947dd5eaa2bcc0b89 - checked `V=1` is still verbose, and no warning on non-Linux.

<!--cf906140f33d8803c4a75a2196329ecb-->
🐙 This pull request conflicts with the target branch and [needs rebase](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#rebasing-changes).

verification.payload:
tree 8ee24e9c4e43d145c3c1ce4d24e01d6971469ada
parent 2d86083fd47011d024c55d15cbaebc089aa23308
author Hennadii Stepanov <32963518+hebasto@users.noreply.github.com> 1781169668 +0100
committer Hennadii Stepanov <32963518+hebasto@users.noreply.github.com> 1781534287 +0100

guix: Fix "Ignoring empty string" CMake warning for non-Linux hosts

Using shell parameter expansion ensures that on non-Linux hosts the
build script won't pass an empty string argument to CMake. This resolves
the "Ignoring empty string" warning.

events.message:
guix: Fix "Ignoring empty string" CMake warning for non-Linux hosts

Using shell parameter expansion ensures that on non-Linux hosts the
build script won't pass an empty string argument to CMake. This resolves
the "Ignoring empty string" warning.

events.body: Rebased to resolve a conflict with the merged bitcoin/bitcoin#35499.

comments.body: 042e490e5f5c9e81f5567ba9c2331e294e0d4cbc: You could add the output/context of what is being fixed, to the commit message.

To be clear, I meant add the actual Guix build output, rather than describing it. i.e:
```bash
HOSTS="arm64-apple-darwin" ./contrib/guix/guix-build
<>
make: Leaving directory '/bitcoin/depends'
CMake Warning:
  Ignoring empty string ("") provided on the command line.


-- The CXX compiler identification is Clang 19.1.7
```
However you can leave this as-is now.
